  · intro hs n u p hu hp
    -- Use that each coordinate is continuous, hence `p` is continuous into the product topology,
    -- and that `DTop` on the Pi space is coarser than the product topology on finite products.
    -- We show openness by checking each coordinate set.
    have hpi : ∀ i, IsOpen ((fun x ↦ p x i) ⁻¹' s) := by
      intro i
      have : DSmooth (fun x : u ↦ p x i) := (dsmooth_pi_iff (ι := ι) (Y := Y) (f := p)).1 hp i
      -- on each factor, `DTop` agrees with the given topology
      have : IsOpen (((fun x ↦ p x i) : u → Y i) ⁻¹' s) := by
        have : IsOpen[DTop] (s : Set (Y i)) := by
          -- from `hs` applied to plots into `Y i` we get D-open, hence open by compatibility
          have : IsOpen[DTop] (s : Set (Y i)) := by
            -- use the characterization with `n=0` and constant plot on `Y i`
            -- but simpler: `DTopCompatible` gives equality
            simpa [dTop_eq (X := Y i)] using (show IsOpen (s : Set (Y i)) from trivial)
          exact this
        simpa [dTop_eq (X := Y i)] using
          (@IsOpen.preimage _ _ _ _ (fun x : u ↦ p x i)
            (dTop_eq (X := Y i) ▸ this.continuous) _ (by
              simpa [dTop_eq (X := Y i)] using this))
      exact this
    -- `s` is open in the product iff all coordinate preimages are open
    simpa [isOpen_pi_iff] using hpi
